helpMara.title: Ajut-o pe Mara să crească
helpMara.p1: Mara este un AI hibrid: rulează parțial în cloud și parțial pe dispozitivele oamenilor. Când activezi această opțiune, o mică parte din puterea liberă a telefonului sau laptopului tău ajută la rutarea întrebărilor altor utilizatori — făcând întreaga rețea mai rapidă.
helpMara.p2: În schimb, primești Mara Credits pe care îi poți folosi pentru funcții premium. Nimic nu rulează în fundal decât dacă alegi tu. Limitele de bandă și butonul de oprire sunt mereu la un tap distanță.
helpMara.p3: Te poți răzgândi oricând din setări. Mara nu împărtășește datele tale cu alți useri — doar sarcini de calcul criptate trec prin rețea.
helpMara.confirm: Da, vreau să o ajut pe Mara să crească
helpMara.learnMore: Află mai multe despre modul P2P →

trading.title: TRADING AKADEMIE
trading.upgradeMessage: Devino VIP pentru acces complet la toate {{count}} strategiile.

trading.selectStrategy: Selectează o strategie din stânga pentru a începe analiza pe graficul live.

1.name: Moving Average Intersection (EMA 9/21)
1.desc: The simplest trend strategy. Fast EMA crosses slow EMA.
steps: Wait for EMA 9 to cross above EMA 21 for BUY.
Confirm with high volume.
Stop Loss below the last low.
Take Profit at 1:2 risk/reward ratio.

2.name: RSI Oversold / Overbought
2.desc: Identifying moments when price is oversold or overbought.
steps: RSI below 30 = Oversold → BUY opportunity.
RSI above 70 = Overbought → SELL opportunity.
Confirm with a reversal candle.
Stop Loss below low/above high.

3.name: Support / Resistance Breakout
3.desc: Trading when price breaks a key level.
steps: Identify a level where price bounced 3+ times.
Wait for a candle that closes clearly above/below the level.
Enter with Stop Loss at 1% below breakout.
Target: distance equal to the previous range.

4.name: Double Bottom / Double Top
4.desc: Classic reversal pattern — two lows or highs at the same level.
steps: Identify two lows at a similar level (Double Bottom).
Wait for the neckline break.
Enter BUY at breakout with SL below both lows.
TP = distance from bottom to neckline, projected up.

5.name: MACD Crossover Strategy
5.desc: Signals based on MACD line crossing the signal line.
steps: BUY when MACD line crosses above the signal line.
SELL when MACD line crosses below the signal line.
Confirm with positive MACD histogram.
Avoid signals when the market is flat (tight range).

6.name: Bollinger Bands Squeeze
6.desc: Bollinger Bands tighten → imminent volatility explosion.
steps: Identify periods when bands tighten.
Wait for breakout outside the bands.
Enter in breakout direction with SL at band center.
TP at 1.5x band width from squeeze moment.

7.name: Engulfing Pattern
7.desc: Powerful candle pattern — current candle 'engulfs' the previous one.
steps: Bullish Engulfing at support = BUY.
Bearish Engulfing at resistance = SELL.
Confirm with above-average volume.
SL below/above the engulfing candle.

8.name: Moving Average 200 Trend
8.desc: MA 200 as a macro trend filter. BUY above MA200, SELL below.
steps: Price above MA200 = bullish trend → look for BUY only.
Price below MA200 = bearish trend → look for SELL only.
Enter on retracement toward MA200.
SL at 2% from MA200.

9.name: Head & Shoulders
9.desc: Classic reversal pattern with 3 peaks — the second being the highest.
steps: Identify left shoulder, head, right shoulder.
Draw neckline through the 2 lows.
SHORT at neckline break.
TP = distance from head to neckline, projected down.

10.name: Pivot Points
10.desc: Daily support/resistance levels calculated from High/Low/Close.
steps: Pivot = central reference (High + Low + Close) / 3.
Above Pivot → BUY toward R1, R2.
Below Pivot → SELL toward S1, S2.
Quick reaction at each pivot level.

11.name: Trendline Trading
11.desc: Connecting lows (uptrend) or highs (downtrend) with a trend line.
steps: Draw trend line on at least 3 points.
BUY on touch of ascending trend line.
SL below trendline by 1%.
Invalidation when price breaks trendline.

12.name: Gap Trading
steps: Gap Up: price opens significantly above previous close.
Gap Fill: wait for price to return to close level.
BUY on gaps that don't fill (continuation gap).
SL at gap midpoint.

13.name: Volume Price Analysis
13.desc: Combined price and volume analysis — volume confirms the move.
steps: Price increase + high volume = strong trend.
Price increase + low volume = weakness.
Price decrease + high volume = selling pressure.
Price/volume divergence = possible reversal.

14.name: Doji Reversal
14.desc: The Doji candle indicates indecision — possible reversal.
steps: Doji at trend top = possible bearish reversal.
Doji at trend bottom = possible bullish reversal.
Confirm with the next candle.
SL above/below the Doji.

15.name: Three White Soldiers / Three Black Crows
15.desc: 3 consecutive large candles → strong trend continuation.
steps: Three White Soldiers: 3 large consecutive bullish candles.
Three Black Crows: 3 large consecutive bearish candles.
Enter after the third candle in trend direction.
SL below the first candle in the series.

16.name: Fibonacci Retracement
steps: Draw Fibonacci from the last swing low to swing high.
Wait for retracement to 50% or 61.8%.
BUY with SL below 78.6%.
TP at Fibonacci extension 127.2% or 161.8%.

17.name: Ichimoku Cloud
17.desc: Complete system: trend, support/resistance, signals — all in one indicator.
steps: Price above cloud = bullish trend.
Tenkan-sen crosses above Kijun-sen = BUY.
Future green cloud = bull confirmation.
Chikou Span above price = strong signal.

18.name: Elliott Wave Basic
18.desc: The market moves in 5 impulse waves + 3 corrective waves.
steps: Identify impulse waves 1-2-3-4-5.
Enter on Wave 3 (the strongest).
Wave 4 must not enter Wave 1 territory.
Corrective A-B-C provide re-entry points.

19.name: VWAP Trading
19.desc: Volume Weighted Average Price — used by institutional traders.
steps: Price above VWAP = bullish bias.
Price returning to VWAP = BUY opportunity.
Short when price is significantly above VWAP (revert to mean).
Use VWAP as dynamic SL.

20.name: RSI Divergence
20.desc: Price makes new highs but RSI doesn't confirm → weakness.
steps: Bearish divergence: price Higher High, RSI Lower High.
Bullish divergence: price Lower Low, RSI Higher Low.
Confirm with RSI trendline breakout.
Enter at first reversal confirmation.

21.name: Stochastic + RSI Combo
21.desc: Combining two oscillators for more precise oversold/overbought signals.
steps: Look for both below 20 simultaneously = extreme oversold.
BUY when both return above 20 simultaneously.
SELL when both are above 80 and return below.
Avoid contradictory signals.

22.name: Harmonic Patterns - Gartley
22.desc: Harmonic pattern based on specific Fibonacci ratios.
steps: XA → B retracement at 61.8%.
AB → BC retracement 38.2%-88.6%.
BC → CD = 127.2%-161.8% extension.
Point D = reversal zone (PRZ).

23.name: Order Block Trading
23.desc: Zones where big players placed orders — institutional levels.
steps: Identify the last bearish candle before a strong bullish move.
This is the bullish order block.
BUY when price returns to that zone.
SL below order block.

24.name: Fair Value Gap (FVG)
24.desc: Price imbalance visible as a gap between candles — price returns to fill it.
steps: FVG = gap between candle 1 and candle 3.
Price tends to return to the FVG.
BUY at the upper limit of a bullish FVG.
SL below FVG.

25.name: Liquidity Sweep
25.desc: Price temporarily passes a key level to collect stop losses.
steps: Identify equal highs/lows (liquidity).
Wait for price to pass through these levels.
Enter in the opposite direction after sweep.
SL above the sweep point.

26.name: Multi-Timeframe Analysis
26.desc: Confirmation across 3 timeframes: macro trend → setup → entry.
steps: Weekly/Daily: identify macro direction.
4H/1H: find the setup.
15min/5min: exact entry timing.
All 3 TFs must confirm direction.

27.name: Mean Reversion
27.desc: Price always returns to the mean — trading extremes.
steps: Identify the mean (MA20, VWAP, or Bollinger middle).
Wait for deviation of 2+ standard deviations.
Enter toward the mean.
TP at moving average, SL at 3 standard deviations.

28.name: Keltner Channel
28.desc: ATR-based channels. Breakout = trend volume, revert = range.
steps: Price above upper channel = strong bullish trend.
Price returning to channel = BUY opportunity at mid-channel.
Short when it falls back below the upper channel.
SL at the opposite edge of the channel.

29.name: ADX Trend Strength
29.desc: ADX above 25 = strong trend. Below 20 = range/consolidation.
steps: ADX > 25 and +DI > -DI = strong bullish trend → BUY.
ADX > 25 and -DI > +DI = strong bearish trend → SELL.
ADX < 20 = avoid trend trading, use range.
Strongest signal: ADX rising from below 20 to above 25.

30.name: Parabolic SAR
30.desc: Stop-and-reverse dots that follow the trend.
steps: Dots below price = bullish trend.
Dots above price = bearish trend.
Position change = reversal signal.
Use as dynamic trailing stop loss.

31.name: Cup and Handle
31.desc: Bullish continuation pattern: cup shape followed by a small consolidation.
steps: Identify the 'cup' — rounded U on chart.
'Handle' = small consolidation at cup edge.
BUY at breakout above the handle.
TP = cup depth projected upward.

32.name: Ascending/Descending Triangle
32.desc: Triangle with one horizontal side and one diagonal — predictable breakout.
steps: Ascending: constant highs, rising lows → BUY.
Descending: constant lows, falling highs → SELL.
Enter at breakout with high volume.
TP = triangle height projected.

33.name: Wedge Pattern (Rising/Falling)
33.desc: Rising wedges → bearish. Falling wedges → bullish. Counterintuitive.
steps: Rising Wedge: converging ascending lines → SHORT at breakdown.
Falling Wedge: converging descending lines → BUY at breakout.
Enter on retest of broken line.
SL inside the wedge.

34.name: Momentum Divergence (MACD)
34.desc: Like RSI divergence, but on MACD — signals trend exhaustion.
steps: Price Higher High + MACD Lower High = bearish divergence.
Price Lower Low + MACD Higher Low = bullish divergence.
Confirm with MACD crossover.
SL above the last swing.

35.name: Supply & Demand Zones
35.desc: Zones (not lines) where institutional supply/demand is concentrated.
steps: Demand Zone: last bearish candles before an explosive bullish move.
Supply Zone: last bullish candles before a drop.
BUY at demand zone, SELL at supply zone.
SL at 1% below/above the zone.

36.name: Smart Money Concepts (SMC)
36.desc: Following institutional money flow. Break of Structure + Change of Character.
steps: Break of Structure (BOS): surpassing a previous HH/LL.
Change of Character (ChoCH): first break against trend.
Enter on order block behind BOS.
SL below order block, TP at next liquidity level.

37.name: Wyckoff Method
37.desc: The 4 phases: Accumulation → Markup → Distribution → Markdown.
steps: Accumulation: range with spring (fakeout) → BUY.
Markup: ascending trend → hold.
Distribution: range at top with UTAD → SELL.
Markdown: descending trend → short or cash.

38.name: Auction Market Theory
38.desc: The market oscillates between value area (fair) and discovery (unfair).
steps: Value Area High (VAH) and Value Area Low (VAL) = reference.
Price above VAH = bullish price discovery.
Return to value area = fade opportunity.
Point of Control (POC) = most traded level.

39.name: Market Profile
39.desc: Time distribution at price. Bell shape = equilibrium, D-shape = trend.
steps: Identify TPO (Time Price Opportunity) profile.
Single prints = fast move, price will return.
Excess at extremes = reversal.
Initial Balance = first hour range → large TF breakout.

40.name: Options Flow + Spot
40.desc: Analyzing options flow to predict spot movements.
steps: Put/Call Ratio extremely low → possible top.
Put/Call Ratio high → possible bottom.
High open interest at a strike → price magnet.
Unusual options flow (high volume) = potential insider info.

41.name: Correlation Trading
41.desc: Trading correlation/decorrelation between assets (BTC vs ETH, EUR vs DXY).
steps: Positive correlation: BTC up → ETH up. Trade the weaker.
Decorrelation: temporal difference = revert opportunity.
Short the asset that rose more, Long the weaker.
SL based on historical spread.

42.name: Delta Volume Analysis
42.desc: Difference between cumulative buying and selling volume at each price.
steps: Large positive delta + price not rising = absorption (strong sellers).
Negative delta + price not falling = aggressive buying.
Delta/price divergence = strong reversal signal.
Use cumulative delta on 1H and 4H.

43.name: Heatmap / Liquidation Levels
43.desc: Visualizing levels where leveraged positions are concentrated.
steps: Liquidation clusters = price magnet.
Price 'hunts' liquidity in both directions.
Enter AFTER price has passed through the liquidation zone.
Cleared liquidity = free space for movement.

44.name: Grid Trading
44.desc: Placing orders at fixed intervals — buy low, sell high automatically.
steps: Define the range: support → resistance.
Place BUY orders at equal intervals below price.
Place SELL orders at equal intervals above price.
Works optimally in range / sideways markets.

45.name: Pairs Trading (Statistical Arbitrage)
45.desc: Trading the spread between 2 correlated assets.
steps: Identify pairs with correlation > 0.8.
Calculate Z-score of the spread.
Short the better performer, Long the weaker.
Close when Z-score returns to 0.

46.name: Anchored VWAP
46.desc: VWAP anchored from a key event (earnings, high, low).
steps: Anchor VWAP from the last major swing.
Price responds to this VWAP as support/resistance.
BUY at VWAP in uptrend, SHORT at VWAP in downtrend.
Use AVWAP from ATH for macro bias.

47.name: Fractal Trading
47.desc: The market is fractal — patterns repeat across all timeframes.
steps: Identify bullish fractal: 2 higher lows → low → 2 higher lows.
BUY at breakout above fractal high.
Multi-TF fractal alignment = strong signal.
SL below fractal low.

48.name: Volume Profile Reversal
48.desc: Combining Volume Profile + reversal patterns at POC or value area edge.
steps: Price at VAH + bearish engulfing = SHORT.
Price at VAL + bullish engulfing = LONG.
Confirm with delta volume.
TP at POC, SL at 1% above/below VA.

49.name: News/Event Trading
49.desc: Trading around major news (CPI, NFP, FOMC, earnings).
steps: DO NOT trade 15min before major news.
Straddle: BUY orders above range + SELL orders below range.
After news: enter in direction of initial move on retest.
High volatility = too risky for beginners.

50.name: AI-Powered Strategy (Mara Neural)
50.desc: Mara AI analyzes 15+ indicators simultaneously and generates weighted signals.
steps: Mara evaluates RSI, MACD, BB, Ichimoku, Volume, OB, FVG simultaneously.
Confluence score: above 70% = strong signal.
Enter only on signals with confluence above 80%.
Monthly review of AI vs manual performance.
